titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/61

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=377 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:17:52.665125+00 | translation moa=378 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:17:54.01879+00

p.cl:
Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis tam presentibus quam futuris . Willelmus de hauecestertun . et Ric’ de haucestertun frater eius . et iohannes filius . alani de Mundegumeri
et Vincencius filius . Roberti auenel . et Nicholaus filius . Rolandi . Salutem .

Sciatis nos dedisse et concessisse . et hac presenti carta nostra confirmasse deo et ecclesie sancte
Marie de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus pro salute animarum nostrarum et pro animabus patrum et matrum et omnium anceteccorum et successroum nostrorum et
pro anima Walteri filio alani domini nostri . et pro animabus omnium fidelium defunctorum quamdam terram in territorio de Inuerwich sicut fassatum circuit eamdem ...
fratribus eorum factum . in liberam et puram et perpetuam elemosinam solutam et quietam ab omni terreno seruicio et exaccione seculari . sicut aliquam elemosinam liberius ...
cius et honorificencius tenent in terra regis scotie . Et nos et heredes nostri warantizabimus predictis Monachis . eamdem terram contra omnes homines inperpetuum . Preter
ea dimisimus eisdem predictis Monachis . communem pasturam eiusdem uille de Inuerewich de profunda uia ad capud de Manineth ad diuisas illas que sunt inter nos
et Monachos de Kelcou . scilicet ex transuerso de monnesclacht usque in Witsclad . et de Witsclad usque in edwardescloth . et edwardescloth usque in botkil . et
sic sursum per eandem aquam scilicet botkil per rectas diuisas de Innerwich usque ad bercariam predictorum monachorum et de illa bercaria usque ad profundam uiam pre
nominatam . Hanc predictam pasturam cum aisiamentis infra predictas diuisas scilicet in petariis . in turbariis . in bruariis tenebunt predicti Monachi de nobis et
heredibus nostris in feudam firmam inperpetuum . libere et quiete et solute de omnibus seruiciis et terrenis exactionibus reddendo inde annuatim nobis et heredibus nostris de
cem solidos ad pentecosten .

Hiis testibus . Willelmo Walensi de staintum . Nigello persona de . uthirglen . Magistro Willelmo de ercheldun . Willelmo cl’ ...
ford Roberto Auenel . Roberto filio . gregorii . Willelmo lefranceis . Ricardo letaillur . Waltero ... et Multis aliis

To all sons of holy Mother Church as well present as future, William of Halkerston, and Richard of Halkerston, his brother, and John son of Alan of Montgomery, and Vincent son of Robert Avenel, and Nicholas son of Roland, send greeting.

us to have given and granted, and by this our present charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of Saint Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God there, for the salvation of our souls, and for the souls of our fathers and mothers and of all our ancestors and successors, and for the soul of Walter son of Alan our lord, and for the souls of all the faithful departed, a land in the territory of Innerwick, as the ditch encircles the same ... made by their brethren, in free, pure, and everlasting alms, loosed and quit of every earthly service and secular exaction, just as they hold any alms freely, quitely and honourably in the land of the king of Scotland. And we and our heirs shall warrant to the aforesaid monks the same land against all men for ever. In addition we have given up to the aforesaid monks the common pasture of the same toun of Innerwick, from the low road to the head of Manineth at the boundaries which are between us and the monks of Kelso, that is, across from Monnesclacht as far as Witsclad, and from Witsclad as far as Edwardescloth, and Edwardescloth as far as Botkil, and so upwards by the same water, that is Botkil, by the correct boundaries of Innerwick as far as the sheep-fold of the aforesaid monks, and from that sheep-fold as far as the low road aforenamed. This aforesaid pasture with easements within the aforesaid boundaries, that is, in peataries, in turbaries, in heathland, the aforesaid monks shall hold of us and our heirs in feu ferme for ever, freely and quitely, released from all services and earthly exactions by returning from it yearly to us and our heirs ten shillings at Pentecost.

With these witnesses: William Wallace of Stenton, Nigel, parson of Rutherglen, Master William of Earlston, William clerk of ...ford, Robert Avenel, Robert son of Gregory, William Francis, Richard Taylor, Walter ... and many others.
